name: Joe Donnelly
subject: Only one year to go.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
With 365 days left, every single thing you do to help us win this race matters. |
---

| | Peter,

It's November 6th. Exactly 1 year from today, Hoosiers will be casting their ballots for who'll represent them in the United States Senate.

Now, I know a year seems like a long time. But it's not that far away. Elections come sooner than you think, and if we're going to be prepared next year, we need to get to work now.

Our campaign is powered by all of us -- a crowd of citizens who want to make sure the voice of ordinary people is heard in the Senate.**The seat that 's up next year belongs to the people of Indiana, and keeping it will rely on all our efforts.**

[**Watch my video about the stakes of this race with one year to go, and then chip in $5 and get involved:**](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c249a/7377f838/352f1a22/51fbacf7/1931053486/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/jd-oneyeartogo?refcode=20171106nd&amount=10") | [](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c249a/7377f838/352f1a22/51fbacf7/1931053486/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/jd-oneyeartogo?refcode=20171106nd&amount=10")
---
This election will be about who has a stronger connection to Hoosiers, and who has a better plan to fight for them on the issues they care about -- more jobs and better schools. Bigger paychecks and a safer nation. Taking care of our veterans, and ensuring quality health care is affordable.

**It 'll be about who listens more closely to working families, and who's willing to work hard, reach across the aisle, and bring a dose of Hoosier common sense to Washington.**

As I run for the Senate again, I'm going to work hard every day to prove that I'm that candidate. But I can't do it alone -- I need your help.

**With 365 days left, every single thing you do to help us win this race matters.** Every door you knock, every phone call you make, every dollar you give -- it all adds up. Every little gesture brings us one step closer to re-election.

[**Watch my video, and then tell me you 're in. We've got a year to go, and not a moment to lose.**](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c249a/7377f838/352f1a22/51fbacf7/1931053486/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/jd-oneyeartogo?refcode=20171106nd&amount=10")

Thanks,
Joe Donnelly

---

name: DNC Voting Info
subject: VIRGINIA: Be sure to vote today!
body:
Today is Election Day.
---
Polls are open, and it's time to vote for Ralph Northam for governor, Justin Fairfax for lieutenant governor, Mark Herring for attorney general, and Democrats up and down the ballot in Virginia.

**Confirm your polling place now, then forward this message to your friends and neighbors so they can go vote, too:**

**[FIND YOUR POLLING PLACE
](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405239aa/4067b0a/406acc71d/7e6681bc/2727065773/VEsH/ "http://my.democrats.org/I-Will-Vote?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+FIND+YOUR+POLLING+PLACE&utm_campaign=em_20171107_dnc_va&source=em_20171107_dnc_va")**
Already cast your ballot? Thanks for being a voter! Make sure every Democrat you know in Virginia knows they can find their polling place at **[IWillVote.com.](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405239aa/4067b0a/406acc71d/7e6681bd/2727065773/VEsE/ "http://my.democrats.org/I-Will-Vote?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+IWillVotecom&utm_campaign=em_20171107_dnc_va&source=em_20171107_dnc_va")**

Thanks,

The Democrats

P.S. -- If you have any questions about voting, give us a call on the DNC Voter Hotline: **[(833) 336-VOTE (8683)](tel:8333368683)**.

Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405239aa/4067b0a/406acc71d/7e6681bb/2727065773/VEsC/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=4+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20171107_dnc_va&source=em_20171107_dnc_va"). This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. name: The White House
subject: Strengthening Resolve, Promoting Freedom
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=32c76170212ea7f46b8003d7ce7be2ab3f6dd1236abad05e4bd284054acd13355254eb552fa90bfdcc87b09c53dddd2f743cc97f112105b8

On the Home Front, Fighting for Tax Reform
The complexity of our current tax code is a burden on American taxpayers, leading to massive frustration, wasted time, and wasted money. President Donald J. Trump is working to reform our tax system to ensure Americans can keep more of their hard-earned money. Tax rates will be substantially reduced, loopholes disproportionately benefitting the wealthy will be eliminated, and most taxpayers will be able to file their taxes on a single page.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=32c76170212ea7f4c691b002873f23a106bc2d856681e19829df21d29ee8e54040ed76b31659e4a41c989bdc754446b5f3a4cc59d02d6e14
Learn more about the pro-job, pro-family, and pro-America Unified Framework for Tax Reform.

President Trump Visits Pyeongtaek, Republic of Korea

Last night, President Donald Trump and First Lady Melania Trump departed Tokyo for Pyeongtaek, Republic of Korea. Before meeting with President Moon Jae-In, the President and First Lady
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=32c76170212ea7f4933305fc4025fb3eb6f845e29bc983bac82799f8fc7a4880492620d5ade0cc5b6e5bd13ca36f2c7277edde679a55eb84
had lunch with service members from the United States and Republic of Korea at Camp Humphreys. Next, they were greeted with an arrival ceremony and a guestbook signing at the Blue House, President Moon’s residence. Later, President Trump participated in a
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=32c76170212ea7f4ef697f84b596a2985e3bb9be9f4620de11b4c9d231b37d928ad9577936b28fe4130ff29932a93c7632683331cbe87818
bilateral meeting , friendship walk, and a
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=32c76170212ea7f4ed454c3ad3368728b5d363ceb71b6b68677a390716e909a54677aaa8fcb82dbf04b4f37362e844336732f7ad6f9b3aa4
joint press conference with President Moon. The President and First Lady attended a state dinner together in the evening.

First Lady Melania Trump in Japan

During her trip to Japan, First Lady Melania Trump joined Mrs. Akie Abe for several cultural events.
After the President and First Lady visited Yokota Air Force Base, Mrs. Trump flew to Tokyo and joined Mrs. Abe for tea and a cultural presentation on the history of the pearl industry. While the President and Prime Minister held a summit on the second day, Mrs. Trump and Mrs. Abe traveled to Kyobashi Tsukiji Elementary School to visit with local school children and receive a traditional calligraphy lesson.

“I enjoyed the time I was able to spend in Japan,” stated First Lady Melania Trump. “It was a pleasure to once again spend time with Mrs. Abe, and discuss many of the issues facing each of our nations. I learned so much about the rich traditions and history of this beautiful country, and want to personally thank Prime Minister and Mrs. Abe for being such gracious hosts.”

Read more about the First Lady’s visit to Japan
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=32c76170212ea7f47e3b487ff2c6ad730fe5540e572570e0f12b896a9a0251bbf5039e19618a771485da6607ce8ab2fb41ade97cb571a4b1
here.

Photo of the Day

First Lady Melania Trump and Mrs. Abe visit with school children in Japan | November 6, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Andrea Hanks)

POTUS TODAY

Today, President Donald Trump and First Lady Melania Trump will participate in an Embassy meet and greet in Seoul, Republic of Korea, followed by a courtesy call with National Assembly leaders. Then, the President will deliver remarks to the National Assembly. After the President's remarks, President Trump and the First Lady will participate in a wreath laying ceremony, and then depart the Republic of Korea en route to Beijing, People's Republic of China. After arriving at the Beijing Airport, the President and First Lady will participate in a welcome tea with President Xi Jinping of the People's Republic of China and Madame Peng Liyuan, followed by a tour of the Forbidden City and viewing of an opera performance. Later, the President and First Lady will have dinner with President Xi and
Madame Peng.

Vice President Pence will travel to the U.S. Capitol to participate in a series of meetings with lawmakers and take part in the Senate Republican Policy Lunch.

COMING UP

Later this week, President Trump will travel to Vietnam and the Philippines, where he will participate in bilateral meetings and continue his Administration's efforts to denuclearize the North Korean regime.

name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: the wealthy and powerful
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
| __ Last week Republicans announced their "tax reform" bill. A close reading quickly reveals that the goal in this tax exercise is less "reform" and more "redistribution" -- the GOP tax bill is a huge giveaway to billionaires and big multinational corporations at the expense of everyone else. And that's only the beginning: After years of lecturing about the dangers of a looming DEBT CRISIS, Paul Ryan and Congressional Republicans are now enthusiastically pushing a bill that will add $1.5 trillion to the national debt. And if it passes, we can be sure that their next act will be to propose massive cuts to programs that help American families in order to address the massive deficit they will have just succeeded in creating. [**The American people cannot afford to be asleep on this one. Republicans are ready to say and do whatever it takes to pass this disastrous bill and we need to raise the alarm bells and fight back. Click here if you agree.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bbf/70cdc2ba/46ba39db/397d21d/1496612981/VEsH/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/stop-republican-giveaways-to-the-wealthy?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+The+American+people+cannot+afford+to+be+&utm_campaign=em110717&source=em110717") Watch what Republicana do and not what they say. The fact is that Republicans are pushing this deficit-increasing bill at the same time as they are refusing to reauthorize the universally supported Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP). Not only does this tax bill significantly raise the national debt by providing big tax breaks for the very richest Americans and large corporations, but it also repeals the state and local income tax deduction -- a move that will double tax hardworking Americans, including 43% of Marylanders. We have seen this trickle-down economics playbook before and we know it doesn't work. When the debt eventually explodes, Republicans will become "deficit hawks" again and demand further cuts in Medicare, Medicaid, Social Security and other programs. [**Raising the national debt by cutting taxes on those who least need it would be a disaster for America. And if it passes, we can be sure that their next act will be to propose massive cuts to programs that help American families in order to address the massive deficit they will have just succeeded in creating. "" Watch what Republicana do and not what they say. The fact is that Republicans are pushing this deficit-increasing bill at the same time as they are refusing to reauthorize the universally supported Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP). Washington clearly didn’t get the message last November. Let’s make sure they hear it from us now: Enough politics as usual. It’s time to get to work

Friend,

One year ago, the American people spoke up against politics as usual and elected the ultimate outsider: Donald Trump.

Since then, the media, Democrats, and the Washington elites have teamed up to obstruct the Trump agenda and destroy his reputation.

And friend, they still don’t realize that by obstructing the Trump agenda, they’re obstructing OUR agenda. We voted for change. We voted to shake up Washington. And the swamp dwellers have done everything they can to maintain Washington’s dysfunction by blocking efforts to repeal Obamacare, build the wall, and cut taxes.

It hasn’t stopped there. At every turn, they’ve relentlessly and personally attacked President Trump.

One year in, I want to remind the president that we still stand with him; that the people still have his back.

|
[](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/120306a/2fde7c66/4480cb18/1569a248/2984167128/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWYVNWQWxKUT09IjoiMjg0NTciLCJKU1ZNUVZOVVRrRk5SU1VsIjoiIn0=/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/don-t-let-republicans-rig-our-elections?&firstname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=28457&lastname=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=em17_171107_ElecRig_adv_gq_ful__RC&source=em17_171107_ElecRig_adv_gq_ful__RC")

Friend,

Republican leaders in the legislature just won't give up on trying to rig the 2018 legislative elections.

When the court wanted a nonpartisan person to redraw the map, Republicans in the legislature continued to fight, asking for another chance to draw their gerrymandered maps. The court rejected their request, saying the legislature had their chance to fix their unconstitutional districts.

They just won't quit.

**[We don't know what else they have up their sleeves, but what we do know is we have to keep fighting for fair and free elections. Add your name here to stay in the fight.](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/120306a/2fde7c66/4480cb18/1569a24b/2984167128/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWYVNWQWxKUT09IjoiMjg0NTciLCJKU1ZNUVZOVVRrRk5SU1VsIjoiIn0=/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/don-t-let-republicans-rig-our-elections?&firstname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=28457&lastname=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&utm_content=2+-+We+dont+know+what+else+they+have+up+thei&utm_campaign=em17_171107_ElecRig_adv_gq_ful__RC&source=em17_171107_ElecRig_adv_gq_ful__RC")**

Having an outside, independent appointee redraw the maps is a significant win for everyone who wants fair elections.

The importance of next year's legislative elections cannot be overstated -- we can significantly change the course of our state with a good showing.

**[I need to know you're with me on this -- add your name today.](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/120306a/2fde7c66/4480cb18/1569a24a/2984167128/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWYVNWQWxKUT09IjoiMjg0NTciLCJKU1ZNUVZOVVRrRk5SU1VsIjoiIn0=/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/don-t-let-republicans-rig-our-elections?&firstname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=28457&lastname=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&utm_content=3+-+I+need+to+know+youre+with+me+on+this++ad&utm_campaign=em17_171107_ElecRig_adv_gq_ful__RC&source=em17_171107_ElecRig_adv_gq_ful__RC")**

Thanks for all you continue to do,

Roy | | [**Add your name ->**](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/120306a/2fde7c66/4480cb18/1569a24d/2984167128/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWYVNWQWxKUT09IjoiMjg0NTciLCJKU1ZNUVZOVVRrRk5SU1VsIjoiIn0=/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/don-t-let-republicans-rig-our-elections?&firstname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=28457&lastname=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&utm_content=4+-+Add+your+name+rarr&utm_campaign=em17_171107_ElecRig_adv_gq_ful__RC&source=em17_171107_ElecRig_adv_gq_ful__RC")

Did you hear, Peter? State Rep. Mike Braun just bought over $320,000 of statewide TV and radio ads to air over the next month. While most Hoosiers are worried about making ends meet, Rep. Braun is just worried about using his wallet to buy his way into the U.S. Senate. We can't let that happen. That's why we're launching a 24-hour Emergency Fightback Fund.

This six-figure ad buy is a big deal, Peter. It's clear that Rep. Braun will spend whatever it takes to try and buy this seat. **Can you chip in $10 today to help us keep Joe in the Senate?**

Rep. Braun has already given more than $800,000 of his own money to his campaign, which is now beginning to blanket the airwaves with ads. This is just the beginning.

There's plenty more where that came from. With a personal net worth of nearly $100 million, Rep. Braun is willing to spend **BIG** to buy this seat. As the race heats up, there is no telling how much further into his pockets he'll dig. Earlier this week he told _Roll Call_ that he thinks he'll need to spend $4 to $6 million just to win the GOP primary!

name: Tom Perez
subject: This is just the beginning, Derek
body:
Democrats won BIG tonight!
---
Because of you, Democrats just won big tonight in Virginia and New Jersey! What we're doing is working, Derek.

This election cycle, the Democratic National Committee invested more in boots on the ground and grassroots and digital organizing than in any "off-year" before. Good old-fashioned organizing paired with the latest technology and tools helped put our candidates over the top.

I am so proud of the campaigns run by Virginia's Ralph Northam, Justin Fairfax, and Mark Herring, New Jersey's Phil Murphy and Sheila Oliver, and Democrats all over the country, up and down the ballot. These candidates worked hard day in and day out fighting to represent their states, and I know that they will take that same spirit and fight into their terms.

But here's the thing: All of this is just the beginning, Derek.

We are going to keep investing in state parties and supporting Democrats from the school board to the Oval Office. And if we continue to channel our energy into powering this movement, there's no doubt in my mind that we will see wins like this in 2018, 2020, and beyond.

**If you're even half as fired up as I am, chip in $10 or more right now to keep electing Democrats.**

**If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately.
